name: slack-approval-workflow
notes:
SETUP GUIDE

1. HOW TO IMPORT
   n8n Settings → Workflows → Import → paste this JSON

2. CREDENTIALS TO CONFIGURE
   - Slack OAuth2: n8n Credentials → New → Slack OAuth2 API
     Paste your Bot Token (xoxb-...) from Slack App → OAuth & Permissions
     Name it exactly: 'Slack Content Pipeline Bot'
   - Supabase API: n8n Credentials → New → Supabase API
     Enter your Supabase project URL and service role key (NOT anon key)
     Name it exactly: 'Supabase Content Engine'

3. SLACK APP SETUP (one-time)
   a. Go to https://api.slack.com/apps → Create New App → From scratch
      App Name: 'Content Pipeline Bot' | Pick your workspace
   b. OAuth & Permissions → Bot Token Scopes → Add: chat:write, chat:write.public
   c. OAuth & Permissions → Install to Workspace → Allow → copy Bot Token
   d. Create #content-pipeline channel in Slack → Invite the bot
   e. Basic Information → App Credentials → copy Signing Secret (for reference)

4. CONFIGURE WORKFLOW PARAMETERS
   - In the 'Flow 1: Send Slack Approval Message' node:
     Set the 'channel' parameter to your #content-pipeline channel ID
     (Right-click channel in Slack → Copy Link → last path segment is channel ID)
   - The Google Sheets URL in the Block Kit message is dynamically constructed
     from the content_bank row — no manual configuration needed

5. REGISTER WEBHOOK URL IN SLACK APP
   - Open the 'Flow 2: Receive Slack Button Callback' webhook node in n8n
   - Copy the webhook URL (shown when workflow is in test/production mode)
   - In your Slack App: Interactivity & Shortcuts → Turn On
   - Paste the webhook URL as the Request URL → Save Changes

6. HOW TO ACTIVATE
   After credential setup and webhook URL registration:
   Toggle the workflow to Active (top-right switch in n8n)

7. HOW TO TEST
   - Manually update any content_bank row status to 'pending_review' in Supabase
   - Within 60 seconds, a Block Kit message should appear in #content-pipeline
   - Click 'Approve All' — content_bank status should change to 'approved' in ~5 seconds
   - No 'Operation failed' error in Slack confirms the 3-second response pattern worked

8. APPROVE SELECTED NOTE
   The 'Approve Selected' button in v1 behaves identically to 'Approve All'.
   A future iteration can add a Slack modal for per-platform selection.
   This simplification is intentional — documented here for the v2 backlog.

9. PUBLISHING HANDOFF
   When Approve All is clicked, this workflow sets content_bank.status='approved' in Supabase.
   Each platform-publisher workflow has its own Supabase trigger that fires on status=approved.
   No manual wiring between this workflow and publishers is needed.
   Ensure all platform-publisher workflows are imported and activated in n8n for end-to-end publishing.

nodes.name: Flow 1: Supabase Trigger (pending_review)
nodes.notes: Watches content_bank table for INSERT and UPDATE events. The IF filter below restricts to status=pending_review rows only.

nodes.name: Flow 1: Filter status = pending_review
nodes.notes: Only proceed when content_bank row status = 'pending_review'. Ignores all other INSERT/UPDATE events to avoid noise in #content-pipeline.

nodes.name: Flow 1: Send Slack Approval Message
nodes.notes: Sends a 4-block Block Kit message to #content-pipeline. Block count: 1 header + 1 image + 1 section + 1 actions = 4 blocks total (well under Slack's 50-block limit). The fallback 'text' field is shown in notifications and accessibility contexts.

nodes.name: Flow 2: Receive Slack Button Callback
nodes.notes: Receives interactive callbacks from Slack when a user clicks any button in the approval message. Slack sends application/x-www-form-urlencoded with a 'payload' field containing JSON. The responseMode=responseNode means the Respond to Webhook node controls the response.

nodes.name: Flow 2: Respond to Webhook (200 OK Immediately)
nodes.notes: CRITICAL: This node MUST fire before any Supabase operations. Slack has a 3-second timeout on interactive callbacks — if no HTTP 200 is received within 3 seconds, Slack shows 'Operation failed' to the user. Sending response_action:clear dismisses any loading state in the Slack UI.

nodes.name: Flow 2: Parse Slack Payload
nodes.notes: Slack sends the interactive callback as application/x-www-form-urlencoded with a single 'payload' field containing URL-encoded JSON. This Set node decodes it and extracts the key fields: action_id (which button was clicked), content_bank_id (the row UUID), user_name, and channel_id for the confirmation message.

nodes.name: Flow 2: Route on action_id
nodes.notes: Routes the callback to the appropriate handler based on action_id. Note: 'edit' action_id is NOT routed here — the Edit in Sheets button uses a url field (opens Google Sheets directly) and does NOT send a callback to this webhook. Approve Selected routes to the same handler as Approve All in v1 — see workflow notes for v2 backlog item.

nodes.name: Flow 2: Supabase Update → status = approved
nodes.notes: Updates content_bank.status to 'approved' for the approved content item. This runs AFTER the Respond to Webhook node has already sent HTTP 200 to Slack — ensuring the 3-second timeout pattern is honored.

nodes.name: Flow 2: Supabase Update → status = rejected
nodes.notes: Updates content_bank.status to 'rejected' for the rejected content item. Runs AFTER the Respond to Webhook node has already sent HTTP 200 to Slack.

nodes.name: Flow 2: Handoff to Publishing Workflow
nodes.notes: Publishing Architecture: When this workflow sets status=approved in Supabase, each platform-publisher workflow (youtube.json, twitter.json, instagram.json, substack.json, threads.json) independently detects the status change via their own Supabase triggers and fires within seconds. No Execute Workflow connection is needed — the event-driven trigger pattern provides independent, fault-tolerant publishing. Each publisher can fail without blocking others.

nodes.name: Flow 2: Slack Confirmation (Approved)

parameters.text: =Content approved by {{ $('Flow 2: Parse Slack Payload').item.json.user_name }}. Publishing handoff queued. (ID: {{ $('Flow 2: Parse Slack Payload').item.json.content_bank_id }})

nodes.notes: Sends a follow-up message to the same channel confirming the approval. This is a separate API call AFTER the Respond to Webhook node — not part of the 3-second response window. The channel_id is captured from the original Slack callback payload.

nodes.name: Flow 2: Slack Confirmation (Rejected)

parameters.text: =Content rejected by {{ $('Flow 2: Parse Slack Payload').item.json.user_name }}. Status set to rejected in content bank. (ID: {{ $('Flow 2: Parse Slack Payload').item.json.content_bank_id }})

nodes.notes: Sends a follow-up message to the same channel confirming the rejection. Separate API call AFTER responding to Slack webhook.

main.node: Flow 2: Respond to Webhook (200 OK Immediately)
